module_notes: SuperComm v2.2 — OS-9 Level II terminal / communications program
v2.2 updates (1992) by Randy K. Wilson
+ CRC-16/CCITT lookup table (L3C0F / CrcTable) for file transfer
+ ZModem support (External ZModem Send/Receive, Auto Zmodem)
+ Module is 3693 bytes larger than v2.1

data_regions.label: CrcTable
data_regions.comment:
CRC-16/CCITT lookup table — 256 entries x 2 bytes = 512 bytes
Used by file transfer protocol routines for fast CRC calculation.
Dave Philipsen added this in v2.2; v2.1 used OS9 F$CRC syscall instead.
Table polynomial: $1021 (CRC-CCITT / CRC-16-IBM-SDLC)
& and quote chars — emitted as FCB to avoid FCC string embedding issues

data_regions.label: Dat_0F18
data_regions.comment: 7-byte data block referenced by LEAX at $0B26 — appears after OS9 F$Exit

data_regions.label: Dat_1750
data_regions.comment: 12-byte orphaned string copy loop (unreferenced from branch analysis)

006F: Splash FORMAT A blocks begin at $0070
1B03: WriteBlock — write count-prefixed block to STDOUT (path=1)
LEAX  DataLabel,PC    ; X → FDB count / data payload
LBSR  WriteBlock      ; LDY [X]++  OS9 I$Write  path=STDOUT
1B05: WriteBlockPath — write count-prefixed block to path already in A
3C0F: CrcTable — CRC-16/CCITT lookup table  (256 × FDB = 512 bytes)
Indexed as: LEAY CrcTable,PC  then LDD B,Y to fetch entry.
Added in v2.2 to replace the slower OS9 F$CRC syscall.

forced_equs.0B8D: mid-instruction entry point -- byte 2 of OS9 I$Close ($10 3F 8F) at $0B8C
forced_equs.19EC: mid-instruction entry point -- byte 2 of LDY #$0001 ($10 8E 00 01) at $19EB; BSR from $1A02 executes LDX #$0001 then falls to OS9 I$Write
forced_equs.3E4E: mid-instruction entry point -- byte 2 of CMPX 80,U ($AC C8 50) at $3E4D; BNE from $3DE0
forced_equs.3BC5: mid-instruction entry point -- byte 1 of LDB 115,U (E6 C8 73) at $3BC4; BEQ from $15382
forced_equs.3F3A: mid-instruction entry point -- byte 1 of LBSR WriteBlock (17 DB C7) at $3F39; BSR from $3F32

03A6.comment: second CurXY byte in Dat_03A4 (two consecutive $02 bytes)
